teaser: Before any journey, story, or new venture, Ganesha is remembered first — the one who clears the path and makes space for what is about to begin.
lesson: Wisdom begins by making space. Remove the small obstacle in front of you before reaching for the large one.
clues: Every new beginning starts by remembering this deity.
Riding a small mouse, this one is vast in wisdom.
A broken tusk became a pen to write a great epic.
An elephant head sits on this remover of obstacles.

teaser: He could leap across oceans and lift mountains, yet his greatest power was the humility to serve.
lesson: True power remembers whom it serves.
clues: A son of the wind, devoted beyond measure.
He carried a whole mountain when he could not find one herb.
He leapt across the ocean to find a captive queen.
His heart is said to hold Rama and Sita within it.

card.title: Hanuman
card.subtitle: Strength That Kneels
card.quote: True power remembers whom it serves.

kathaTitle: The promise that cost a kingdom
teaser: On the eve of his crowning, a promise made long ago sent him into fourteen years of exile — and he went without bitterness.
lesson: A promise becomes sacred when it costs something.
clues: The prince of Ayodhya who chose exile over the throne.
His wife and brother walked into the forest beside him.
A devoted vanara army helped him cross the sea.
His name is the heart of the Ramayana.

card.title: Rama
card.subtitle: The Prince of Dharma
card.quote: A promise becomes sacred when it costs something.

teaser: On a battlefield of doubt, he refused to fight himself yet guided a hesitating warrior toward his duty.
lesson: Act with focus on the action, not anxious grasping at the result.
clues: A flute-player loved by the cowherds of Vrindavan.
He lifted a hill to shelter a village from a storm.
He was the charioteer who would not lift a weapon.
His counsel on the battlefield became the Bhagavad Gita.

card.title: Krishna
card.subtitle: The Charioteer of Choice
card.quote: Do your work; release the result.

teaser: Feared for the hardships he brings, he is in truth a patient teacher — returning to us exactly what we have earned.
lesson: Delay can become discipline. What is true does not need to hurry.
clues: A slow-moving graha, the teacher of patience.
Even a great king once braced himself before this gaze.
He is said to make karma visible.
Saturday carries his name.

card.title: Shani Dev
card.subtitle: The Slow Teacher
card.quote: What is true does not need to hurry.

kathaTitle: The mother who rides a lion
teaser: When no single god could defeat the shape-shifting demon, their combined light took form as a fierce and radiant mother.
lesson: Gentleness and ferocity can live in the same heart.
clues: A goddess who rides a lion into battle.
She was formed from the combined power of the devas.
She defeated a buffalo-headed demon none other could.
Nine nights of Navratri celebrate her.

card.title: Durga
card.subtitle: The Mother of Power
card.quote: Gentleness and ferocity can share one heart.

teaser: Seated on a white lotus with a veena in hand, she is the quiet source of music, learning, and clear speech.
lesson: Knowledge flows like a river — keep it moving and it stays clear.
clues: A goddess dressed in white, seated on a lotus.
She holds a veena and a book.
A swan is her companion.
Students seek her blessing for learning and music.

card.title: Saraswati
card.subtitle: The River of Speech
card.quote: Knowledge stays clear only when it keeps moving.

kathaTitle: Neither man nor beast, neither day nor night
teaser: A boon made a tyrant nearly unkillable — so protection arrived in a form that slipped through every loophole of his pride.
lesson: Devotion is protected in ways arrogance can never foresee.
clues: An avatar of Vishnu who is half-lion, half-man.
He appeared at twilight — neither day nor night.
He came to protect the devoted boy Prahlada.
He emerged from a pillar to end a tyrant's boon.

card.title: Narasimha
card.subtitle: The Form Between Forms
card.quote: Devotion is guarded in ways pride cannot foresee.
